title: podman run option to preserve cgroup to detect OOM
body:
### Feature request description

When container OOMs, we need to reliably detect whether it is OOM, or some other problem.

### Suggest potential solution

`podman run --preserve-cgroup-on-termination`. This command would keep cgroup after container terminated for any reason, so cgroup can be inspected for OOM or for other metrics.

AFAIU, this requires patching not only `podman`, but also `conmon`?

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I have not found a reliable way to detect OOM after container terminated.

### Additional context

Using with gVisor if that matters.

comments.body:
Hi @stepancheg 

I'm confused by your request, could you please clarify what you mean by `keep cgroup after container terminated`?

Podman has a way to detect if a container exits due to OOM:

Here is a small reproducer: 
```sh
podman run --memory=100m --cgroup-conf=memory.oom.group=1 --name oomtest fedora:42 sh -c "tail /dev/zero"
podman inspect oomtest --format {{.State.OOMKilled}}
```

@ninja-quokka is correct, that is the reliable way to detect a container exit due to OOM.

`--preserve-cgroup-on-termination` does not only require changes in Podman, but in the OCI runtime as well and I am not even sure how that would be possible to achieve with systemd, since the scope is automatically cleaned up when the main process exits.

> podman inspect oomtest --format {{.State.OOMKilled}}

TIL, thanks!

> I am not even sure how that would be possible to achieve with systemd, since the scope is automatically cleaned up when the main process exits.

I see, thanks!
